mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.8%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth outpaces housing starts growth indicates supply-side resilience amid weakening demand, which historically supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth minus HOUST growth) shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, rising real activity measured by GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to correlate positively with 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion, which sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher during months with positive PAYEMS growth compared to contraction months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises above 205.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL breaches the 205.0 threshold despite elevated FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in real personal savings indicates a demand shock driven by household balance sheet deterioration, which historically precedes equity drawdowns and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of rising UNRATE and falling RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in walk-forward testing (Rank IC > -0.02).

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases where industrial production growth exceeds 2.0% quarter-over-quarter, corporate margin expansion accelerates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2.0% fails to correlate with positive 5-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%.
